commands.review_summary:
The PR removes the OpenShell plugin dependency on npm `openshell`, updates CLI/backend helpers for standalone CLI and gateway endpoint handling, makes sandbox names Kubernetes-safe, stabilizes sandbox backend registration across duplicate module loads, and adds focused regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, but not from a live run in this review. Current main still depends on npm `openshell` and resolves `openshell/package.json` before PATH fallback, matching the linked installed-package failure path.
commands.review_followup: The PR is paired with an open linked issue, has a protected maintainer label, and changes dependency/security-sensitive package resolution, so the next action is maintainer review rather than automated cleanup.

target.title: Fix OpenShell sandbox backend CLI resolution
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #56813.

This removes the accidental dependency on the unrelated npm `openshell` package and keeps the OpenShell sandbox backend pointed at the real standalone OpenShell CLI when no bundled command is available.

It also tightens the OpenShell path by:
- falling back to `openshell` on `PATH` when no bundled OpenShell command exists
- passing configured gateway endpoints through OpenShell CLI and SSH proxy flows
- making OpenShell sandbox names Kubernetes-safe
- keeping sandbox backend registrations stable across duplicated module loads
- adding targeted regression coverage for the OpenShell backend and CLI helpers

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: OpenClaw's OpenShell sandbox backend no longer resolves the unrelated npm `openshell@0.1.0` Telegram bot package, which required `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN` and caused `exec` tool startup failures.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix-openshell`; focused OpenShell backend tests run through the repo Vitest wrapper.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openshell/src/backend.test.ts extensions/openshell/src/openshell-core.test.ts`
`git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

Evidence after fix: The targeted OpenShell backend/CLI regression tests passed, including fallback-to-PATH behavior when no bundled OpenShell command is present.

Observed result after fix: The test command exited successfully, and whitespace validation passed.

OpenClaw + standalone OpenShell screenshot proof will be added to this PR after the current OC run completes.
target.author: sallyom
